req.title: Hr-Admin deletes employee
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to delete a employee through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
Here's a comprehensive pytest test suite for the employee deletion requirement:

```python
import pytest
import json
from unittest.mock import patch, MagicMock, call
from datetime import datetime, timezone

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Assumptions / conventions
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# DELETE /api/v1/employees/{employee_id}
# HTTP 204  – success (no body)
# HTTP 400  – validation error          (missing / invalid mandatory fields)
# HTTP 401  – unauthenticated
# HTTP 403  – authenticated but wrong role
# HTTP 404  – employee not found
# HTTP 409  – business-rule conflict    (e.g. employee has open dependencies)
# HTTP 500  – unexpected server error
#
# Mandatory request fields (passed as JSON body or query params depending on
# your spec – here we use a JSON body so the caller must supply a reason and
# confirm the target employee_id):
#   - employee_id  (path parameter)
#   - reason       (body – mandatory audit/business field)
#   - confirmed_by (body – hr-admin user id, must match auth token)
#
# Audit record fields that MUST be written on success:
#   - action        = "DELETE_EMPLOYEE"
#   - target_id     = <employee_id>
#   - performed_by  = <hr-admin user id>
#   - reason        = <reason from request>
#   - timestamp     = ISO-8601 UTC
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


# ── Fixtures ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

@pytest.fixture
def app():
    """Create and configure the Flask/FastAPI test application."""
    from myapp import create_app  # replace with your actual import
    application = create_app({"TESTING": True, "DATABASE_URI": "sqlite:///:memory:"})
    yield application


@pytest.fixture
def client(app):
    """Test client."""
    with app.test_client() as c:
        yield c


@pytest.fixture
def hr_admin_token():
    """Valid JWT for an hr-admin user."""
    return "Bearer valid-hr-admin-jwt-token"


@pytest.fixture
def non_admin_token():
    """Valid JWT for a regular employee (no hr-admin role)."""
    return "Bearer valid-employee-jwt-token"


@pytest.fixture
def hr_admin_user():
    return {"id": "usr-001", "username": "jane.admin", "role": "hr-admin"}


@pytest.fixture
def existing_employee():
    return {
        "id": "emp-123",
        "first_name": "John",
        "last_name": "Doe",
        "email": "john.doe@example.com",
        "department": "Engineering",
        "status": "active",
    }


@pytest.fixture
def valid_delete_payload(hr_admin_user, existing_employee):
    return {
        "reason": "Position eliminated during restructuring",
        "confirmed_by": hr_admin_user["id"],
    }


@pytest.fixture
def mock_employee_service():
    with patch("myapp.services.employee_service") as mock:
        yield mock


@pytest.fixture
def mock_audit_service():
    with patch("myapp.services.audit_service") as mock:
        yield mock


@pytest.fixture
def mock_auth():
    """Patch the auth decorator to inject a known hr-admin principal."""
    with patch("myapp.auth.decode_token") as mock:
        mock.return_value = {"id": "usr-001", "role": "hr-admin", "username": "jane.admin"}
        yield mock


# ── Helper ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def delete_employee(client, employee_id, payload, token):
    return client.delete(
        f"/api/v1/employees/{employee_id}",
        data=json.dumps(payload),
        content_type="application/json",
        headers={"Authorization": token},
    )


# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 1. AUTHENTICATION TESTS
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

class TestAuthentication:

    def test_delete_without_token_returns_401(self, client, existing_employee, valid_delete_payload):
        """No Authorization header → 401 Unauthorized."""
        response = client.delete(
            f"/api/v1/employees/{existing_employee['id']}",
            data=json.dumps(valid_delete_payload),
            content_type="application/json",
        )
        assert response.status_code == 401

    def test_delete_with_expired_token_returns_401(
        self, client, existing_employee, valid_delete_payload
    ):
        """Expired JWT → 401 Unauthorized."""
        response = delete_employee(
            client,
            existing_employee["id"],
            valid_delete_payload,
            "Bearer expired-jwt-token",
        )
        assert response.status_code == 401

    def test_delete_with_malformed_token_returns_401(
        self, client, existing_employee, valid_delete_payload
    ):
        """Malformed token → 401 Unauthorized."""
        response = delete_employee(
            client,
            existing_employee["id"],
            valid_delete_payload,
            "Bearer not.a.valid.token",
        )
        assert response.status_code == 401

    def test_delete_with_invalid_signature_returns_401(
        self, client, existing_employee, valid_delete_payload
    ):
        """Token with tampered signature → 401."""
        response = delete_employee(
            client,
            existing_employee["id"],
            valid_delete_payload,
            "Bearer header.payload.bad_signature",
        )
        assert response.status_code == 401

    def test_error_response_does_not_leak_internals_on_auth_failure(
        self, client, existing_employee, valid_delete_payload
    ):
        """401 body must not expose stack traces or internal details."""
        response = delete_employee(
            client,
            existing_employee["id"],
            valid_delete_payload,
            "Bearer bad-token",
        )
        body = response.get_json()
        assert "stack" not in str(body).lower()
        assert "traceback" not in str(body).lower()


# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 2. AUTHORISATION TESTS
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

class TestAuthorisation:

    def test_delete_by_non_admin_returns_403(
        self, client, existing_employee, valid_delete_payload, non_admin_token
    ):
        """Authenticated user without hr-admin role → 403 Forbidden."""
        response = delete_employee(
            client,
            existing_employee["id"],
            valid_delete_payload,
            non_admin_token,
        )
        assert response.status_code == 403

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("role", ["employee", "manager", "recruiter", "read-only"])
    def test_delete_by_insufficient_roles_returns_403(
        self, client, existing_employee, valid_delete_payload, role
    ):
        """Any role other than hr-admin must be denied."""
        with patch("myapp.auth.decode_token") as mock_token:
            mock_token.return_value = {"id": "usr-999", "role": role}
            response = delete_employee(
                client,
                existing_employee["id"],
                valid_delete_payload,
                "Bearer some-token",
            )
        assert response.status_code == 403

    def test_forbidden_response_contains_error_message(
        self, client, existing_employee, valid_delete_payload, non_admin_token
    ):
        """403 response body should explain the denial."""
        response = delete_employee(
            client,
            existing_employee["id"],
            valid_delete_payload,
            non_admin_token,
        )
        body = response.get_json()
        assert "error" in body or "message" in body or "detail" in body

    def test_hr_admin_can_delete_employee(
        self,
        client,
        existing_employee,
        valid_delete_payload,
        hr_admin_token,
        mock_employee_service,
        mock_audit_service,
        mock_auth,
    ):
        """Happy-path: hr-admin role → 204."""
        mock_employee_service.get_by_id.return_value = existing_employee
        mock_employee_service.delete.return_value = True
        mock_audit_service.write.return_value = None

        response = delete_employee(
            client,
            existing_employee["id"],
            valid_delete_payload,
            hr_admin_token,
        )
        assert response.status_code == 204


# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 3. MANDATORY FIELD VALIDATION TESTS
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

class TestMandatoryFieldValidation:

    def test_missing_reason_returns_400(
        self, client, existing_employee, hr_admin_token, mock_auth
    ):
        """'reason' is mandatory – omitting it must return 400."""
        payload = {"confirmed_by": "usr-001"}  # no reason
        response = delete_employee(client, existing_employee["id"], payload, hr_admin_token)
        assert response.status_code == 400

    def test_empty_reason_returns_400(
        self, client, existing_employee, hr_admin_token, mock_auth
    ):
        """'reason' must not be an empty string."""
        payload = {"reason": "", "confirmed_by": "usr-001"}
        response = delete_employee(client, existing_employee["id"], payload, hr_admin_token)
        assert response.status_code == 400

    def test_whitespace_only_reason_returns_400(
        self, client, existing_employee, hr_admin_token, mock_auth
    ):
        """'reason' containing only whitespace is treated as empty."""
        payload = {"reason": "   ", "confirmed_by": "usr-001"}
        response = delete_employee(client, existing_employee["id"], payload, hr_admin_token)
        assert response.status_code == 400

    def test_missing_confirmed_by_returns_400(
        self, client, existing_employee, hr_admin_token, mock_auth
    ):
        """'confirmed_by' is mandatory."""
        payload = {"reason": "Position eliminated"}  # no confirmed_by
        response = delete_employee(client, existing_employee["id"], payload, hr_admin_token)
        assert response.status_code == 400

    def test_empty_confirmed_by_returns_400(
        self, client, existing_employee, hr_admin_token, mock_auth
    ):
        payload = {"reason": "Position eliminated", "confirmed_by": ""}
        response = delete_employee(client, existing_employee["id"], payload, hr_admin_token)
        assert response.status_code == 400

    def test_confirmed_by_mismatch_returns_400(
        self, client, existing_employee, hr_admin_token, mock_auth
    ):
        """confirmed_by must match the authenticated user's id."""
        payload = {"reason": "Position eliminated", "confirmed_by": "usr-DIFFERENT"}
        response = delete_employee(client, existing_employee["id"], payload, hr_admin_token)
        assert response.status_code == 400

    def test_completely_empty_body_returns_400(
        self, client, existing_employee, hr_admin_token, mock_auth
    ):
        """Sending no body at all must return 400."""
        response = client.delete(
            f"/api/v1/employees/{existing_employee['id']}",
            data="",
            content_type="application/json",
            headers={"Authorization": hr_admin_token},
        )
        assert response.status_code == 400

    def test_non_json_content_type_returns_400(
        self, client, existing_employee, hr_admin_token, mock_auth
    ):
        """Wrong Content-Type must be rejected."""
        response = client.delete(
            f"/api/v1/employees/{existing_employee['id']}",
            data="reason=test&confirmed_by=usr-001",
            content_type="application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
            headers={"Authorization": hr_admin_token},
        )
        assert response.status_code == 400

    def test_invalid_employee_id_format_returns_400(
        self, client, hr_admin_token, valid_delete_payload, mock_auth
    ):
        """Path parameter employee_id must match the expected format."""
        response = delete_employee(
            client, "not-a-valid-!!-id@@", valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token
        )
        assert response.status_code == 400

    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "bad_payload, description",
        [
            ({"reason": None, "confirmed_by": "usr-001"}, "null reason"),
            ({"reason": 12345, "confirmed_by": "usr-001"}, "numeric reason"),
            ({"reason": ["list"], "confirmed_by": "usr-001"}, "list as reason"),
            ({"reason": "ok", "confirmed_by": None}, "null confirmed_by"),
            ({"reason": "ok", "confirmed_by": 999}, "numeric confirmed_by"),
        ],
    )
    def test_invalid_field_types_return_400(
        self,
        client,
        existing_employee,
        hr_admin_token,
        mock_auth,
        bad_payload,
        description,
    ):
        """Wrong types for mandatory fields must be rejected with 400."""
        response = delete_employee(
            client, existing_employee["id"], bad_payload, hr_admin_token
        )
        assert response.status_code == 400, f"Failed for: {description}"

    def test_400_response_body_identifies_missing_fields(
        self, client, existing_employee, hr_admin_token, mock_auth
    ):
        """400 body should list which fields failed validation."""
        payload = {"confirmed_by": "usr-001"}  # missing reason
        response = delete_employee(client, existing_employee["id"], payload, hr_admin_token)
        body = response.get_json()
        body_str = json.dumps(body).lower()
        assert "reason" in body_str

    def test_400_response_body_is_valid_json(
        self, client, existing_employee, hr_admin_token, mock_auth
    ):
        payload = {}
        response = delete_employee(client, existing_employee["id"], payload, hr_admin_token)
        assert response.status_code == 400
        assert response.get_json() is not None


# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 4. RESOURCE NOT FOUND TESTS
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

class TestResourceNotFound:

    def test_delete_nonexistent_employee_returns_404(
        self,
        client,
        hr_admin_token,
        valid_delete_payload,
        mock_auth,
        mock_employee_service,
    ):
        """Deleting an employee that does not exist → 404."""
        mock_employee_service.get_by_id.return_value = None
        response = delete_employee(
            client, "emp-DOES-NOT-EXIST", valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token
        )
        assert response.status_code == 404

    def test_404_body_contains_meaningful_message(
        self,
        client,
        hr_admin_token,
        valid_delete_payload,
        mock_auth,
        mock_employee_service,
    ):
        mock_employee_service.get_by_id.return_value = None
        response = delete_employee(
            client, "emp-DOES-NOT-EXIST", valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token
        )
        body = response.get_json()
        assert body is not None
        assert "error" in body or "message" in body or "detail" in body

    def test_no_audit_record_written_when_employee_not_found(
        self,
        client,
        hr_admin_token,
        valid_delete_payload,
        mock_auth,
        mock_employee_service,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """Audit must NOT be written when the operation fails."""
        mock_employee_service.get_by_id.return_value = None
        delete_employee(
            client, "emp-DOES-NOT-EXIST", valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token
        )
        mock_audit_service.write.assert_not_called()


# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 5. SUCCESSFUL DELETION TESTS
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

class TestSuccessfulDeletion:

    @pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
    def setup_happy_path(
        self,
        mock_auth,
        mock_employee_service,
        mock_audit_service,
        existing_employee,
    ):
        mock_employee_service.get_by_id.return_value = existing_employee
        mock_employee_service.delete.return_value = True
        mock_audit_service.write.return_value = None

    def test_successful_delete_returns_204(
        self,
        client,
        existing_employee,
        valid_delete_payload,
        hr_admin_token,
    ):
        response = delete_employee(
            client, existing_employee["id"], valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token
        )
        assert response.status_code == 204

    def test_successful_delete_returns_no_body(
        self,
        client,
        existing_employee,
        valid_delete_payload,
        hr_admin_token,
    ):
        """204 responses must have no body."""
        response = delete_employee(
            client, existing_employee["id"], valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token
        )
        assert response.status_code == 204
        assert response.data == b"" or response.content_length in (None, 0)

    def test_employee_service_delete_is_called_with_correct_id(
        self,
        client,
        existing_employee,
        valid_delete_payload,
        hr_admin_token,
        mock_employee_service,
    ):
        delete_employee(
            client, existing_employee["id"], valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token
        )
        mock_employee_service.delete.assert_called_once_with(existing_employee["id"])

    def test_deleted_employee_no_longer_retrievable(
        self,
        client,
        existing_employee,
        valid_delete_payload,
        hr_admin_token,
        mock_employee_service,
    ):
        """After deletion the service must not return the employee."""
        delete_employee(
            client, existing_employee["id"], valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token
        )
        mock_employee_service.get_by_id.return_value = None
        response = client.get(
            f"/api/v1/employees/{existing_employee['id']}",
            headers={"Authorization": hr_admin_token},
        )
        assert response.status_code == 404

    def test_idempotency_second_delete_returns_404(
        self,
        client,
        existing_employee,
        valid_delete_payload,
        hr_admin_token,
        mock_employee_service,
    ):
        """Deleting the same employee twice must return 404 on the second call."""
        delete_employee(
            client, existing_employee["id"], valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token
        )
        mock_employee_service.get_by_id.return_value = None
        response = delete_employee(
            client, existing_employee["id"], valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token
        )
        assert response.status_code == 404


# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 6. AUDIT RECORD TESTS  ← core requirement
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

class TestAuditRecord:

    @pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
    def setup(
        self,
        mock_auth,
        mock_employee_service,
        mock_audit_service,
        existing_employee,
        hr_admin_user,
    ):
        self.employee = existing_employee
        self.admin = hr_admin_user
        mock_employee_service.get_by_id.return_value = existing_employee
        mock_employee_service.delete.return_value = True
        mock_audit_service.write.return_value = None
        self.audit = mock_audit_service

    def test_audit_record_is_written_on_success(
        self, client, valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token
    ):
        delete_employee(client, self.employee["id"], valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token)
        self.audit.write.assert_called_once()

    def test_audit_record_action_is_delete_employee(
        self, client, valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token
    ):
        delete_employee(client, self.employee["id"], valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token)
        _, kwargs = self.audit.write.call_args
        record = kwargs.get("record") or self.audit.write.call_args[0][0]
        assert record["action"] == "DELETE_EMPLOYEE"

    def test_audit_record_contains_correct_target_id(
        self, client, valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token
    ):
        delete_employee(client, self.employee["id"], valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token)
        _, kwargs = self.audit.write.call_args
        record = kwargs.get("record") or self.audit.write.call_args[0][0]
        assert record["target_id"] == self.employee["id"]

    def test_audit_record_contains_performer_id(
        self, client, valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token
    ):
        delete_employee(client, self.employee["id"], valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token)
        _, kwargs = self.audit.write.call_args
        record = kwargs.get("record") or self.audit.write.call_args[0][0]
        assert record["performed_by"] == self.admin["id"]

    def test_audit_record_contains_reason(
        self, client, valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token
    ):
        delete_employee(client, self.employee["id"], valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token)
        _, kwargs = self.audit.write.call_args
        record = kwargs.get("record") or self.audit.write.call_args[0][0]
        assert record["reason"] == valid_delete_payload["reason"]

    def test_audit_record_contains_timestamp(
        self, client, valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token
    ):
        delete_employee(client, self.employee["id"], valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token)
        _, kwargs = self.audit.write.call_args
        record = kwargs.get("record") or self.audit.write.call_args[0][0]
        assert "timestamp" in record

    def test_audit_timestamp_is_iso8601_utc(
        self, client, valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token
    ):
        delete_employee(client, self.employee["id"], valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token)
        _, kwargs = self.audit.write.call_args
        record = kwargs.get("record") or self.audit.write.call_args[0][0]
        ts = record["timestamp"]
        # Must parse without error and be UTC-aware
        parsed = datetime.fromisoformat(ts)
        assert parsed.tzinfo is not None

    def test_audit_written_before_response_is_returned(
        self, client, valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token
    ):
        """Audit write must happen within the same request, not deferred."""
        call_order = []
        self.audit.write.side_effect = lambda *a, **kw: call_order.append("audit")

        with patch("myapp.services.employee_service.delete") as mock_del:
            mock_del.side_effect = lambda *a, **kw: call_order.append("delete") or True
            delete_employee(
                client, self.employee["id"], valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token
            )

        assert "audit" in call_order, "Audit service was never called"
        assert call_order.index("delete") < call_order.index("audit"), (
            "Audit should be written after the delete but still in the same request"
        )

    def test_no_audit_written_on_validation_failure(
        self, client, hr_admin_token
    ):
        """Audit must NOT be written when request validation fails."""
        bad_payload = {"confirmed_by": "usr-001"}  # missing reason
        delete_employee(client, self.employee["id"], bad_payload, hr_admin_token)
        self.audit.write.assert_not_called()

    def test_no_audit_written_on_auth_failure(
        self, client, valid_delete_payload
    ):
        """Audit must NOT be written on authentication failure."""
        response = client.delete(
            f"/api/v1/employees/{self.employee['id']}",
            data=json.dumps(valid_delete_payload),
            content_type="application/json",
        )
        assert response.status_code == 401
        self.audit.write.assert_not_called()

    def test_no_audit_written_on_authorisation_failure(
        self, client, valid_delete_payload, non_admin_token
    ):
        """Audit must NOT be written on authorisation failure."""
        delete_employee(
            client, self.employee["id"], valid_delete_payload, non_admin_token
        )
        self.audit.write.assert_not_called()

    def test_delete_rolls_back_if_audit_write_fails(
        self, client, valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token, mock_employee_service
    ):
        """If the audit write fails the delete must be rolled back (atomicity)."""
        self.audit.write.side_effect = Exception("Audit store unavailable")
        response = delete_employee(
            client, self.employee["id"], valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token
        )
        # Transaction must not commit
        assert response.status_code == 500
        mock_employee_service.delete.assert_called_once()
        # Verify rollback was triggered (depends on your implementation)
        mock_employee_service.rollback.assert_called_once()

    def test_exactly_one_audit_record_per_deletion(
        self, client, valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token
    ):
        """Exactly one audit record per successful delete – no duplicates."""
        delete_employee(client, self.employee["id"], valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token)
        assert self.audit.write.call_count == 1


# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 7. EDGE & CONFLICT CASES
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

class TestEdgeCases:

    def test_delete_already_deleted_employee_returns_404(
        self,
        client,
        hr_admin_token,
        valid_delete_payload,
        mock_auth,
        mock_employee_service,
    ):
        """Soft-deleted employee should not be found (404)."""
        mock_employee_service.get_by_id.return_value = None
        response = delete_employee(
            client, "emp-already-deleted", valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token
        )
        assert response.status_code == 404

    def test_delete_employee_with_open_dependencies_returns_409(
        self,
        client,
        existing_employee,
        hr_admin_token,
        valid_delete_payload,
        mock_auth,
        mock_employee_service,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """Employee with open payroll runs / active contracts → 409 Conflict."""
        mock_employee_service.get_by_id.return_value = existing_employee
        mock_employee_service.delete.side_effect = Exception("ConflictError: open dependencies")
        response = delete_employee(
            client, existing_employee["id"], valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token
        )
        assert response.status_code == 409
        mock_audit_service.write.assert_not_called()

    def test_database_error_returns_500(
        self,
        client,
        existing_employee,
        hr_admin_token,
        valid_delete_payload,
        mock_auth,
        mock_employee_service,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """Unexpected database error → 500, no audit record."""
        mock_employee_service.get_by_id.return_value = existing_employee
        mock_employee_service.delete.side_effect = Exception("DB connection lost")
        response = delete_employee(
            client, existing_employee["id"], valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token
        )
        assert response.status_code == 500
        mock_audit_service.write.assert_not_called()

    def test_500_response_does_not_leak_stack_trace(
        self,
        client,
        existing_employee,
        hr_admin_token,
        valid_delete_payload,

        mock_employee_service,
    ):
        mock_employee_service.get_by_id.return_value = existing_employee
        mock_employee_service.delete.side_effect = Exception("Internal error")
        response = delete_employee(
            client, existing_employee["id"], valid_delete_payload, hr_admin_token
        )
        body = json.dumps(response.get_json()).lower()
        assert "traceback" not in body
        assert "exception" not in body

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("method", ["GET", "POST", "PUT", "PATCH"])
    def test_wrong_http_methods_return_405(
        self, client, existing_employee, hr_admin_token
    ):
        """Only DELETE is allowed on this endpoint."""

            method=method,
            headers={"Authorization": hr_admin_token},
        )
        assert response.status_code == 405

    def test_sql_injection_in_employee_id_returns_400(
        self,
        client,
        hr_admin_token,
        valid_delete_payload,
        mock_auth,
    ):
        response = delete_employee(
            client,
            "1; DROP TABLE employees; --",
            valid_delete_payload,
            hr_admin_token,
        )
        assert response.status_code == 400
